name: Module-Lattice-Based Key-Encapsulation Mechanism Standard
owner: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
description: FIPS 203 (2024) standardizes ML-KEM (Module-Lattice-Based Key Encapsulation Mechanism, based on CRYSTALS-Kyber) — the first NIST post-quantum cryptography standard for key encapsulation. Replaces classical key-establishment mechanisms vulnerable to quantum computers.
relationToCloudSecurity: Cloud KMS, TLS, and VPN services are migrating to FIPS 203 over the next decade.
relationToAISecurity: AI model-IP protection and federated-learning communications can benefit from PQC adoption.

license.notes: Work of NIST is in the US public domain (17 USC §105).

current_extraction.notes: Metadata-only stub. Source PDF acquisition + extraction is TODO.
